upstream_context.intent: Pin and operationalize externally referenced standards into deterministic local-model rules

directives.title: Normative Keyword Discipline
directives.rationale: AES rules depend on unambiguous requirement language. Treat MUST/SHALL as merge-blocking unless waiver metadata is present.
implementation_patterns: Classify statements into MUST|SHOULD|MAY before execution planning.
Reject downgrading MUST to SHOULD during summarization.
Require waiver tuple: rationale + risk acceptance + approver + expiry.
verification_checks: All normative statements are tagged with severity class.
Any MUST violation without waiver metadata fails validation.
Generated summaries preserve normative modality.

directives.title: ASVS Security Verification Floor
directives.rationale: AES references OWASP ASVS as the baseline verification model; exposed or sensitive components need explicit ASVS-level handling.
implementation_patterns: Default externally reachable components to ASVS L2 control coverage.
Escalate authentication/authorization/high-value data paths to ASVS L3 intent.
Attach control mapping evidence to review artifacts.
verification_checks: Security checklist includes ASVS control IDs or equivalent mapped controls.
Authentication/authorization paths include elevated assurance evidence.
Unmapped exposed endpoint is treated as policy failure.

directives.title: CWE Top 25 Risk Blocking
directives.rationale: AES baselines require explicit mitigation for prevalent software weakness classes.
implementation_patterns: Map changed components to relevant CWE Top 25 categories.
Configure scanners to promote relevant CWE hits to blocking severity for high-assurance code.
Require mitigation note or false-positive disposition for each blocking CWE class.
verification_checks: CWE mapping exists for every changed externally reachable module.
Blocking CWE findings cannot be auto-waived.
Mitigation status is attached to change metadata.

directives.title: NIST Control-Family Mapping
directives.rationale: AES requires security controls to align to a recognized catalog, with SP 800-53 Rev. 5 used as baseline.
implementation_patterns: Tag each security requirement to at least one SP 800-53 family (AC/AU/CM/SC minimum set).
Store control-family tags in machine-readable review output.
Treat untagged high-risk controls as incomplete design evidence.
verification_checks: Control-family tags exist for all security-critical requirements.
Audit artifacts show traceable mapping from requirement to control family.
Missing AC/AU/CM/SC consideration fails readiness check.

directives.title: Supply-Chain Provenance Minimum
directives.rationale: AES baselines set SLSA Level 3 provenance expectations for high-assurance outputs.
implementation_patterns: Emit provenance records for every release artifact with builder identity and immutable inputs.
Enforce dependency pinning and integrity metadata in lockfiles.
Preserve provenance links in release notes and audit logs.
verification_checks: Release artifact has attached provenance attestation.
Build input digests are reproducible from recorded metadata.
Unsigned or provenance-missing critical artifact is blocked.

directives.title: OpenTelemetry Structured Telemetry Baseline
directives.rationale: AES requires structured observability with trace context propagation and semantic consistency.
implementation_patterns: Emit logs/metrics/traces with consistent service and operation attributes.
Propagate trace and span identifiers across service boundaries.
Use histogram metrics for latency distributions used in release gates.
verification_checks: Telemetry events contain correlation identifiers.
Schema fields match selected semantic-convention version.
Latency gate evaluates p95/p99 from histogram-compatible metrics.

directives.title: Pinned-Baseline Change Control
directives.rationale: AES baseline updates must be deliberate to prevent policy drift and uncontrolled enforcement changes.
implementation_patterns: Keep policy pins explicit (ASVS, CWE, NIST, SLSA, OpenTelemetry).
Require impact analysis and migration tickets before baseline bumps.
Record explicit cross-owner signoff for baseline changes.
verification_checks: Baseline diff exists for each upstream version bump.
Mapped rule impacts are listed before merge.
Unsigned baseline bump is rejected.
